function.name: execute
function.description: Run a command in the workspace and return its exit code, stdout and stderr. High-risk (approved by the human in Ask mode; runs autonomously in Auto). Prefer run_command for the project's declared test/build/lint steps. For a LONG-RUNNING process (a server/watcher), set background:true with a goal — you get a job_id back immediately; poll it with process_output and stop it with stop_process when done.

argv.description: Command as an array, no shell — e.g. ["python3", "script.py"]. Use this OR shell+command.

shell.description: If true, run `command` through bash -c (enables pipes, &&, redirects, globs).

command.description: Shell command string; only used when shell is true.

cwd.description: Optional working directory, relative to the workspace root (defaults to the root).

background.description: Run as a long-lived BACKGROUND process: returns a job_id immediately instead of waiting. Requires goal. It is auto-killed at its deadline, when this run ends, or if you stop polling it.

goal.description: Required when background: WHY the process is running (e.g. 'serve the app on :3000 so I can run the e2e tests').

ready_when.description: Optional readiness gate for background — the call returns once it holds: {"port": 3000} | {"log": "<regex>"} | {"http": "<url>"}.

max_lifetime_sec.description: Optional hard lifetime for a background process (seconds; default 600, max 7200). Killed at this deadline no matter what.

scope.description: Background lifetime scope: 'run' (default — killed when this agent run ends) or 'session' (survives across runs; you must stop it).
